items.title: Storms force evacuation of July 4 celebrations in Washington
items.description: Trump to speak at Lincoln Memorial as extreme heat and thunderstorms disrupt 250th anniversary events

items.title: Iran mourners demand revenge at ayatollah’s funeral
items.description: The week-long remembrance is intended to demonstrate public support for the regime

items.title: My fellow American: who should be remembered for the next 250 years?
items.description: Artists, thinkers and leaders from the US and beyond pick the person they think deserves to be honoured, from Abraham Lincoln to Dorothy Parker

items.title: Francis Fukuyama: The problem of equality
items.description: We must understand our identity to be fundamentally rooted in a set of ideas, not the racial or ethnic identity of our ancestors

items.title: The massacre America forgot
items.description: The events of 1919 had largely been erased in the town of Elaine, Arkansas. Now a new generation is documenting the legacy of racial conflict for itself

items.title: Yiyun Li: ‘Moby-Dick’ is the Great American Novel of pursuit and destruction
items.description: Like its country of origin, the book is unabashed, sometimes self-indulgent, often brilliant and never subdued or apologetic

items.title: Simon Schama: The founding fathers and the battle for America’s future
items.description: In 1776, visions of liberty and justice were fraught with compromise, conflict and contradiction. Today, the founders’ words still shape national identity

items.title: Yankee pyramids: how to build a presidential library
items.description: Every modern US president gets a physical totem of his tenure. What can they teach us about America’s struggle to remember itself?
